official_ifs_requirements.requirement: Competition closes Tuesday 28 July 2026 at 11:00am; UK registered academic institutions can apply for a share of up to GBP800,000.
Project total eligible costs across Phase 1a and Phase 1b must be between GBP5,000 and GBP32,000.
Eligible project costs are salaries of academics participating in the programme plus travel and subsistence to ACTASAP Phase 1 events only.

To work alone or lead, the organisation must be based in a UK registered academic institution, have an ACT-related idea, and have support from the institution technology transfer office or equivalent.
Funded collaborators must be UK registered academic institutions; non-funded partners can participate from their own resources but do not receive funding.
Subcontractors are not allowed in this competition.
Lead applicant must make sure the proposal meets eligibility/scope, all sections are complete, and collaborators accept terms before submission.

local_evidence_readback.finding: ACTASAP is recorded as ACADEMIC_LEAD_REQUIRED, not R&A direct-submit ready.
Matrix states ACTASAP requires a UK registered academic institution lead and should not be submitted by R&A.
Plan records ACTASAP as academic institution lead only.
QUB and Ulster are outreach/affiliation targets only. No confirmed ACTASAP academic lead, TTO support, or IFS invitation found.
Only a QUB outreach email copy appeared. No ACTASAP, CyberASAP, TTO, or advanced-connectivity academic lead evidence found.

drive_searches.result_summary: Returned RA Grant War Room package files only. The inspected text classifies ACTASAP as academic-institutions-only and not directly viable for R&A.
Returned writing/docx and unrelated technical markdown files. No confirmed academic lead, TTO support, or ACTASAP IFS invitation found.
Returned RA Grant War Room package files only. No TTO support letter or academic institution lead evidence found.

drive_files_inspected.title: RA_Grant_War_Room_Package.md
drive_files_inspected.finding: Planning package classifies ACTASAP as academic institutions only and R&A as a business; it does not evidence a confirmed academic lead, TTO support, or IFS invitation.

route_action.reason: Official ACTASAP rules require a UK academic institution lead and TTO/equivalent support. Current Aureon evidence does not confirm those gates.

next_actions: Do not submit ACTASAP directly as R&A; R&A is not the eligible lead for this competition.
Only change route to draft-ready if a UK academic institution confirms lead status, ACT-related academic project ownership, TTO support, and an IFS invite or collaboration role.
If R&A participates, treat it as a non-funded partner unless the official rules and lead institution confirm a funded eligible academic route.
Exclude internal language, unsupported market claims, bank details, tax references, and private contact details from any formal ACTASAP document.
controls: No unsupported academic lead claim made
No unsupported TTO support claim made
No unsupported funded-partner or non-funded-partner invite claim made
No IFS portal mutation, external email, upload, partner invite, or final submission performed
